06_ALT_status_tel_ins

Andrea Polazzi

2026-03-18

Setup

library(readxl)
source('../scripts/clean_columns.R')
data <- read_xlsx('../data/raw/PCAWG_variables_PCA_woK.xlsx')
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1255 / R1255C9: got 'NA'
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1724 / R1724C9: got 'NA'
clean_data <- clean_pcawg_data(data)
model_data_reduced <- clean_data[complete.cases(clean_data), -c(1,2,5)]  # we remove also telomere_insertion_rate
summary(model_data_reduced)
##     alt_status     tf_rate         telomere_content_log2   TERT_FPKM

##                 3rd Qu.: 0.07243   3rd Qu.:-0.08914      3rd Qu.: 0.39077  
##                 Max.   :19.73776   Max.   : 3.84469      Max.   :53.76790

##  1st Qu.:-0.56491      1st Qu.:-0.2686       1st Qu.:-0.32314

PCA without tel. insertion

X <- scale(model_data_reduced[, -1])
pca <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
summary(pca)
## Importance of components:
##                           PC1    PC2    PC3     PC4     PC5     PC6     PC7
## Standard deviation     1.6826 1.2127 1.1311 0.97563 0.93360 0.92652 0.88095
## Proportion of Variance 0.2359 0.1226 0.1066 0.07932 0.07263 0.07154 0.06467
## Cumulative Proportion  0.2359 0.3585 0.4651 0.54442 0.61706 0.68859 0.75327
##                            PC8     PC9    PC10   PC11    PC12
## Standard deviation     0.84985 0.80408 0.75989 0.7324 0.69147
## Proportion of Variance 0.06019 0.05388 0.04812 0.0447 0.03984
## Cumulative Proportion  0.81345 0.86733 0.91545 0.9602 1.00000
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA without tel. insertion",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  )

pca$rotation
##                               PC1         PC2         PC3         PC4
## tf_rate               -0.18050723 -0.20152254 -0.34965740  0.04083258
## telomere_content_log2 -0.16446516  0.12579057 -0.63331402 -0.02575951
## TERT_FPKM             -0.04877422  0.37827525 -0.46774374 -0.42911277
## ATAGGG_singleton_dist -0.26771644  0.26615485  0.21965844 -0.12417006

Biplot

# sample coordinates
scores <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

# motif vectors
loadings <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$rotation[,1],
  PC2 = pca$rotation[,2],
  comp_names = c(rownames(pca$rotation)[1:3], sapply(strsplit(rownames(pca$rotation)[4:12], split = '_', fixed = TRUE), `[`, 1))
)

# scale the loadings (smaller than sample coordinates) to make arrows more visible
loadings$PC1 <- loadings$PC1 * 10
loadings$PC2 <- loadings$PC2 * 10

library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
## Warning: package 'ggrepel' was built under R version 4.5.2
p <- ggplot(scores, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +

  geom_segment(data = loadings,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25,"cm")),
               inherit.aes = FALSE,
               color = "black") +

  geom_text_repel(data = loadings,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, label = comp_names),
                  inherit.aes = FALSE,
                  size = 3.5) +

  theme_classic() +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA biplot without tel. insertion",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  )

plot(p)

ggsave("../outputs/PCA_biplot_red_telins.pdf", plot = p, width = 7, height = 6)

Comparing the two PCA biplots, the most evident change after removing telomere insertion rate is the reduction of the strong directional signal toward the lower-left quadrant. In the full-feature PCA, the telomere insertion rate vector is long and points strongly in that direction, and many ALT-high tumors lie along this axis, indicating that this variable contributes substantially to the separation between ALT-high and ALT-low samples. Once this feature is removed, the PCA structure becomes more centered and the remaining variables distribute more evenly around the origin. The ALT-high samples still tend to occupy the lower-left region of the plot, but the separation from ALT-low tumors becomes less pronounced. The remaining vectors, particularly TGAGGG, TTCGGG, GTAGGG, and TF rate—continue to point toward this region, suggesting that these variables now capture part of the variance previously dominated by telomere insertion rate.

Split the dataset

set.seed(21)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(model_data_reduced), size=0.7*nrow(model_data_reduced))
train_red <- model_data_reduced[train_index,]
test_red <- model_data_reduced[-train_index,]

# Check distribution
table(train_red$alt_status, dnn = 'train')
## train
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      537       34
table(test_red$alt_status, dnn = 'test')
## test
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      231       15

First classifier: Logistic Regression

logreg_model_reduced <- glm(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  family = "binomial"
)

prob_red <- predict(
  logreg_model_reduced,
  test_red,
  type = "response"
)

Evaluate performance

library(pROC, quietly = T)
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
roc_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  prob_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_red, main = 'ROC curve - Log. Reg. (all features except tel. insertion)')

auc(roc_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.9143
prob_df <- data.frame(
  prob = prob_red,
  alt_status = test_red$alt_status
)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(prob_df, aes(x = prob, fill = alt_status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted probability distributions",
    x = "Predicted probability of ALT-high",
    y = "Density"
  )

Other metrics

pred_class <- ifelse(prob_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
pred_class <- factor(pred_class, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret, quietly = TRUE)
## Warning: package 'lattice' was built under R version 4.5.2
confusionMatrix(pred_class, test_red$alt_status, positive = 'ALT-high')
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      230        5
##   ALT-high       1       10
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.9756         
##                  95% CI : (0.9477, 0.991)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.006358       
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.7567         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.220671       
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.66667        
##             Specificity : 0.99567        
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.90909        
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97872        
##              Prevalence : 0.06098        
##          Detection Rate : 0.04065        
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.04472        
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.83117        
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high       
##

When telomere insertion rate is removed, the logistic regression model still achieves strong predictive performance. The model reaches an AUC of 0.914, indicating excellent discrimination between ALT-high and ALT-low tumors even without the strongest structural telomere feature. The confusion matrix shows a high overall accuracy of 97.6% and a balanced accuracy of 0.83, reflecting strong performance despite the class imbalance. The model maintains very high specificity (0.996), meaning that ALT-low tumors are almost never misclassified, while sensitivity is moderate (0.67), indicating that roughly two thirds of ALT-high tumors are correctly detected. The positive predictive value of 0.91 further indicates that most tumors predicted as ALT-high are true positives.

Second classifier: Random Forest

library(randomForest, quietly = T)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
rf_red <- randomForest(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_pred_red <- predict(
  rf_red,
  test_red,
  type = "prob"
)[,"ALT-high"]

Evaluate performance

roc_rf_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  rf_pred_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_rf_red, main = 'ROC curve - RF (all features except tel. insertion')

auc(roc_rf_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.9088

Other metrics

rf_pred_red <- ifelse(rf_pred_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
rf_pred_red <- factor(rf_pred_red, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret)

confusionMatrix(
  rf_pred_red,
  test_red$alt_status,
  positive = "ALT-high"
)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      231        6
##   ALT-high       0        9
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.9756         
##                  95% CI : (0.9477, 0.991)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.006358       
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.738          
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.041227       
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.60000        
##             Specificity : 1.00000        
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.00000        
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97468        
##              Prevalence : 0.06098        
##          Detection Rate : 0.03659        
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.03659        
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.80000        
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high       
##

When telomere insertion rate is removed, the Random Forest classifier still achieves strong discriminatory performance, with an AUC of approximately 0.91. This is very similar to the logistic regression reduced model and indicates that the remaining predictors—TF rate, telomere content, TERT expression, and TVR motifs—retain substantial information about ALT status. The confusion matrix shows a high overall accuracy (97.6%) and a balanced accuracy of 0.80, confirming that the model remains highly effective despite the removal of the strongest structural telomere feature.

The model exhibits perfect specificity (1.00), meaning that ALT-low tumors are never misclassified as ALT-high in this split. However, sensitivity is moderate (0.60), indicating that some ALT-high tumors remain difficult to detect when telomere insertion rate is excluded. The positive predictive value of 1.00 shows that when the model predicts ALT-high, the prediction is always correct in this dataset. Overall, the model remains highly reliable but detects slightly fewer ALT-high cases compared with the full model.

varImpPlot(rf_red, main = 'Variable importance plot - Random Forest (all features except tel. insertion)' )

After removing telomere insertion, the PCA shows increased overlap between ALT-high and ALT-low samples, indicating that the remaining features provide weaker but still meaningful separation. This is reflected in the classification results, where the AUC decreases slightly (≈0.91) but remains high. The Random Forest variable importance plot further supports this interpretation: in the absence of telomere insertion rate, the most influential predictors become specific TVR motifs—particularly TTTGGG, TGAGGG, and TTCGGG followed by TF rate and GTAGGG. These features are also oriented toward the region of the PCA space where many ALT-high tumors cluster, indicating that they capture the remaining signal associated with ALT status.

Together, the PCA and the classifier results therefore suggest that while telomere insertion rate is a dominant driver of discrimination, a combination of TVR motifs and other telomere-related features still provides substantial predictive information about the ALT status.

Cross Validation of the performances

To assess the robustness of these two predictive models, we conducted repeated stratified cross-validation.

Logistic Regression - CV

library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    logreg_cv_model <- glm(alt_status~., data = train_cv, family = 'binomial')
    
    logreg_cv_prob <- predict(logreg_cv_model, test_cv, type = 'response')
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = logreg_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.8976512
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results

Random Forest - CV

library(randomForest)
library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    rf_cv_model <- randomForest(
      alt_status ~ .,
      data = train_cv,
      ntree = 500,
      mtry = 2
    )
    
    rf_cv_prob <- predict(rf_cv_model, test_cv, type = "prob")[, "ALT-high"]
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = rf_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.9192728
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results

These results confirm the robustness of the AUC values previously recorded for the two models, and agree with the fact that allowing non-linear relationships among features (i.e. using Random Forest) provide a better classification power.
